title: Offer system
description: POST /offers (buyer constructs offer PSBT at custom price), GET /offers?inscription_id=x (list offers), POST /offers/:id/accept (seller accepts, triggers buy flow), DELETE /offers/:id (buyer cancels). Offers expire after configurable TTL.

title: ord node integration service
description: Service to query local ord node REST API (default port 80). Methods: get_inscription(id), get_inscriptions_by_address(addr), get_sat_info(sat), get_inscription_content(id), get_collection(id). Handle ord API response parsing into internal Rust types.

title: Mempool watcher - confirmation tracking
description: Background tokio task polling Bitcoin RPC every 30s for pending sale txids. On confirmation: update listing status to sold, update inscription owner in DB, record sale in activity_feed, update collection floor/volume stats. Handle reorg edge cases.

title: Database schema and migrations
description: PostgreSQL schema with SQLx migrations for tables: inscriptions, listings, sales, collections, offers, activity_feed. Include indexes for owner address, inscription_id, status queries.

title: WebSocket real-time events
description: Axum WebSocket endpoint /ws. Push events to connected clients: new_listing, sale_confirmed, offer_received, price_update. Use tokio broadcast channel internally. Clients subscribe by inscription_id or collection_id.

title: Rust backend project scaffold
description: Initialize Cargo workspace with Axum, SQLx, bitcoincore-rpc, bitcoin (rust-bitcoin) crates. Set up project structure: src/routes, src/services, src/models, src/db. Configure .env for Bitcoin node RPC, ord node URL, PostgreSQL connection.

title: Listing API routes
description: Axum routes for listing management: POST /listings (create listing, returns PSBT), POST /listings/:id/confirm (submit signed PSBT), GET /listings (browse with filters: collection, price range, status), GET /listings/:id, DELETE /listings/:id (cancel). Auth via signed message from wallet.

title: Inscription indexer sync
description: Background tokio task: poll local ord node for new inscriptions and ownership changes every new block (detect via Bitcoin RPC getblockcount polling or ZMQ). Upsert inscriptions and ownership into DB. Track transfer events for activity feed.

title: Order/Buy API routes
description: Axum routes for buying: POST /orders (initiate buy, returns PSBT to sign), POST /orders/:id/confirm (submit buyer-signed PSBT, triggers broadcast), GET /orders/:id (status). Handle double-spend race condition with DB-level locking on listing.

title: Activity feed and stats API routes
description: GET /activity (recent sales/listings/transfers with pagination), GET /collections/:id/stats (floor, volume_24h, sales_count, owner_count), GET /inscriptions/:id/history. Computed from activity_feed and sales tables.

title: Bitmap Marketplace Rust Backend
description: Full Rust backend for a Bitcoin Ordinals marketplace. Routes to local Bitcoin Core node (JSON-RPC) and local ord node. Axum web framework, SQLx + PostgreSQL, rust-bitcoin for PSBT construction. Covers: inscription indexing, trustless PSBT trading (list/buy/offer), collection stats, activity feed, mempool confirmation watching, real-time WebSocket events, royalties.

title: PSBT service - buy flow completion
description: Complete buyer-side PSBT flow: (1) fetch seller's signed PSBT from DB, (2) verify inscription UTXO still unspent via Bitcoin RPC, (3) add buyer payment inputs + inscription output + change output, (4) calculate fee using current fee rate, (5) return to frontend for buyer signing, (6) validate complete PSBT, (7) broadcast via Bitcoin RPC.

title: Bitcoin Core RPC service
description: Rust service layer wrapping bitcoincore-rpc crate. Methods: get_raw_transaction, get_utxo_info, broadcast_transaction, get_block_count, estimate_fee_rate, validate_address. Connect to local Bitcoin node via RPC credentials from config.

title: Royalty enforcement service
description: Off-chain royalty deduction in PSBT construction. When building buyer PSBT, add royalty output to creator address (% of sale price). Royalty metadata stored per collection. Applied during both direct buy and offer acceptance flows.

title: PSBT service - listing construction
description: Build PSBT for seller listings using rust-bitcoin. Flow: (1) fetch inscription UTXO via Bitcoin RPC, (2) construct PSBT with inscription input + payment output, (3) set SIGHASH_SINGLE | ANYONECANPAY on seller input, (4) return base64 PSBT to frontend for wallet signing, (5) validate returned signed PSBT.

title: Collections and inscriptions API routes
description: GET /inscriptions/:id, GET /inscriptions/:id/content (proxy content from ord), GET /collections, GET /collections/:id, GET /collections/:id/inscriptions. Include sat rarity, traits, floor price, volume stats.
